channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Cautions China Over Support to Pakistan’s Military Operations
item.description: New Delhi has urged China to carefully consider the reputational consequences of providing support to Pakistan, which is widely regarded as a state that sponsors terrorist activities. This comes after...

item.title: Nottingham Alt-Country Band Divorce Continues as a Two-Piece After Line-Up Changes
item.description: Nottingham alt-country band Divorce has announced that they will continue as a two-piece following the departure of drummer Kasper Sandstrom and lead guitarist Adam Peter Smith, who are stepping away ...

item.title: Director of RGS Group faces fraud charges in Tongaat Hulett business rescue case
item.description: Aquil Rajahussen, director of the Mozambican-based RGS Group, appeared in Randburg Magistrate’s Court on Tuesday facing fraud charges linked to Tongaat Hulett’s business rescue bidding process. Rajahu...

item.title: Profile of Wes Streeting, Labour politician seeking party leadership
item.description: Wes Streeting, a prominent figure within the Labour Party, has announced his intention to vie for the leadership of the party. Known for his approachable and charismatic personality, Streeting hails f...

item.title: Top 5 Nigerian Universities With Most Applications in 2026
item.description: As the 2026 JAMB admission season don waka finish, e clear say students dey choose universities based on more than just prestige. Lagos State University (LASU) top the list again with 84,426 first-cho...

item.title: South Africa begins impeachment process against President Ramaphosa over Farmgate scandal
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa is now facing impeachment proceedings linked to the so-called 'Farmgate' scandal, where a significant sum of foreign cash was stolen from his ranch. Ramaphosa, ...

item.title: U.S.-Israel Conflict Highlights Strains in Global Petrodollar System
item.description: The ongoing U.S.-Israel military actions against Iran are revealing vulnerabilities in the global petrodollar system. For the first time outside the pandemic, U.S. public debt has surpassed national G...

item.title: Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei Remains Out of Public View Amid Health Concerns
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, has remained largely out of public view since being injured in an airstrike on February 28, which coincided with the outbreak of the Iran-U.S. war. T...

item.title: Pere Egbi Shares Final Conversation With Alex Ekubo Before His Passing
item.description: Naija News don report say former Big Brother Naija housemate, Pere Egbi, don talk about him last conversation with popular Nollywood actor, Alex Ekubo, before him die. For emotional post wey Pere drop...

item.title: Precautions to Reduce Hantavirus Risk During Home and Cottage Cleaning
item.description: As Canadians begin spring cleaning in their homes and cottages, Health Canada advises taking precautions against hantavirus, a virus primarily carried by rodents such as deer mice, cotton rats, rice r...

item.title: Labour Party Divisions Deepen Amid Calls for Starmer to Resign
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is facing a turbulent period as multiple members of his Cabinet resign and pressure grows from within the Labour Party for him to step down. Health Secretary Wes Streeting, alongside ...

item.title: UN Chief António Guterres Urges African Self-Reliance and Local Solutions
item.description: United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres has emphasised that Africa should not be seen as a passive continent waiting for external solutions. Speaking on current challenges facing African nat...

item.title: Judiciary Pledges Full Support to Newly Appointed Judges to Enhance Efficiency
item.description: The Chief Registrar of Kenya's Judiciary, Winfridah Mokaya, has reassured the newly appointed judges of the High Court and Environment and Land Court that they will receive full administrative and ope...

item.title: Tanzania Sets Ambitious 2026/27 Agriculture Targets with Focus on Cash Crops and Avocado Exports
item.description: Tanzania Ministry of Agriculture don announce new targets for the 2026/27 farming year, aiming to lift traditional cash crop production from 1,599,945.66 tonnes in 2025/26 to 2,118,000 tonnes, which i...

item.title: US Spending on Iran Conflict Approaches $29 Billion, Pentagon Reports
item.description: The Pentagon has revealed that the United States' financial expenditure in its ongoing military operations related to Iran is approaching $29 billion. This figure represents the cumulative costs of de...

item.title: ICC explains that national authorities are responsible for executing arrest warrants
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) emphasized that it depends on national governments to enforce its arrest warrants. This statement came after the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) tried to ...

item.title: New Jersey Republican Congressman Remains Absent Due to Unspecified Illness
item.description: New Jersey Republican Congressman Tom Kean Jr. has been absent from Capitol Hill for over two months due to an unspecified medical condition. Kean has not voted since March 5, and his office has only ...

item.title: Edo State Police Arrest 12 Suspected Cult Members During Initiation Ceremony
item.description: Edo State Special Security Squad don arrest 12 suspected cult members during one initiation ceremony for Benin City. Police say dem no directly involve for one recent student killing near UNIBEN gate,...

item.title: Sowah misses Simba U-20 training session after returning to squad
item.description: After being reinstated to the Simba U-20 squad, Sowah did not show up for training as expected. His absence has sparked curiosity among fans and sports followers, since reinstatement usually signals a...

item.title: Gachagua Seeks Supreme Court Ruling to Maintain High Court Impeachment Bench
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has approached the Supreme Court aiming to dismiss an appeal challenging the composition of the High Court bench handling impeachment petitions. According to s...
